name: Cheese Triangles
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the shredded jack cheese (or cottage cheese), eggs, chopped parsley, and salt in a bowl. Mix until well combined.
2. Cut each sheet of phyllo dough in half lengthwise. Work with one sheet at a time, keeping the rest covered with a damp towel until ready to use.
3. Brush each half with melted butter, then fold it twice vertically to form a narrow strip.
4. Place about 1 tablespoon of cheese mixture at one end of each strip. Fold one corner across the filling to form a triangle shape.
5. Brush more butter onto the triangles and continue folding them flag-style (one layer at a time) while brushing with additional butter.
6. Place the finished triangles on baking sheets lined with parchment paper or foil.
7. Bake in a preheated oven at 350°F for about 15 to 20 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown.
